rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BARIBEAU VS. HARRY C. THOMAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Defendant Pacific Union International, Inc. And John Farnhams Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT LAURA ROGERS, BROWN & CO. REAL ESTATE, INC. Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Defendant Pacific Union International, Inc. And John Farnhams Counsel GRANTED on both grounds advanced by the moving party. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Pacific Union International, Inc. shall have 30 days to file a substitution of counsel. Any pending deadlines for Pacific Union International, Inc. are stayed until the substitution is filed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: NELLIE BERTUCCI ET AL VS. ANGELIQUE TAVARES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF ROBERT BERTUCCI MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS GRANTED. Cross-Complainant has no standing to maintain causes of action for conversion and FC 1101 at this time. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY HOUGH ET AL VS. TODD COURAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents From Airbnb; Request For Sanctions Against Aibnb In The Amount Of $1,717
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF WENDY HOUGH, MATTHEW HRUDKA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents From Airbnb; Request For Sanctions Against Aibnb In The Amount Of $1,717 Hearing Required. Parties to appear at 8:30 a.m. an engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. Parties to report of their progress at 9:30 a.m. Additionally, parties to address: (1) why documents regarding the price of the rental/rental listing amount shall not be produced as "documents concerning or relating to short term rentals of residential unit;" and (2) why the documents provided and e-mail instructions are not sufficient to obtain the information sought. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN MANHAN ET AL VS. SHANNON GALLAGHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT SHANNON GALLAGHER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect This matter was continued solely for the purpose of addressing the referral of Plaintiff's counsel to the Bar. The Court determines that based on the explanations provided in responsive declarations there was no intentional misrepresentation. The Court will not refer this matter to the Bar at this time. Counsel is admonished regarding representations to the Court without full understating of the facts or law.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MCLAUGHLIN FAMILY ASSOCIATES, LP ET AL VS. MAXIMUS 620 OWNER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT MCLAUGHLIN FAMILY ASSOCIATES, LP, EJC FOLSOM LLC DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for restitution/unjust enrichment. Based on the allegations in the Fifth Amended Complaint this claim is not ripe, i.e. there is no benefit that Plaintiff has/is currently retaining. Cross-complainant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that permitted shared access to the entitlement documents coupled with a hypothetical eventuality that they may be printed out and submitted to the City is sufficient to rise to the level of receiving and retaining a benefit. At the heart of the dispute raised by the allegations of the cross-complaint (and arguments in opposition) is the interpretation of paragraph 4.3 of PSA. The parties have a different understanding and interpretation of this provision. Cross-Complainant shall have a leave to amend to allege a cause of action for declaratory relief only regarding parties' rights and obligations under this provision. Any such cross-complaint shall be filed and served no later than July 19, 2019. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK A STATE CHARTERED BANK VS. RENKA PROP. LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL

rulings.Case Title: OLUSOJI FANOIKI VS. 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 7. Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed continued to July 12, 2019. Plaintiff to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the Response to OSC no later than June 24, 2019 with a cover reflecting new hearing date. Additionally, no later than June 28, 2019 Plaintiff shall file a declaration stating that all due and owing assessments as of June 27, 2019 have been paid and listing all assessments that are owing but are not yet due, including the due dates. Thereafter, on the 28th day of each month Plaintiff shall file a similar declaration. Courtesy copies of these declarations shall be delivered to department 501 within 1 business day of filing. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. TRAVIS ABERNATHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 21, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT TRAVIS ABERNATHY, STEVEN SABIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment DENIED. Defendants have failed to meet their initial burden under CCP §437c to demonstrate that the unlawful detainer has no merit or that there is a complete defense to it. Travis Abernathy's declarations are not deemed sufficient to indisputably establish the material fact that the unit was not used for illegal purposes. (CCP § 437c(e).) Moreover, Defendants have not offered any legal basis supporting that FEHA, FHAA, or any other cited law creates an affirmative defense to an unlawful detainer action pursuant to CCP §1161(4)/SFRO 37.9(b)(4). Plaintiff's Objections to Abernathy Decl. 6, 7 are SUSTAINED for lack of foundation and as otherwise being inadmissible hearsay. (Cal. Evid. Code §§702, 1200.) Plaintiff's Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff has not cited a single statute or case that would support its request for sanctions. = (501/CFH)
